No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on financial computing and programming skills could indirectly contribute to developing financial services, aligning minimally with Target 1.4 (access to financial services).

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly develops technical and problem-solving skills for employment in finance (Target 4.4).
evidence: 提高學生的程式能力和模型建構能力

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly develops advanced financial computing skills, supporting technological upgrading (Target 8.2), decent job creation (Target 8.3), and strengthening financial institutions (Target 8.10).
evidence: 提高學生的程式能力和模型建構能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly enhances technological capabilities and encourages innovation in the financial industry through the application of computer science and C++ programming (Target 9.5).
evidence: 運用數學、統計、電腦科學等知識

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The advanced financial computing and model-building skills taught are foundational for improving the regulation and monitoring of financial markets (Target 10.5), indirectly contributing to reduced inequalities.
evidence: 金融模型的實現與演算法的設計

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency D, 'understand and identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' provides a minimal, inferred link to encouraging sustainable practices (Target 12.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course content relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Financial computing skills can be applied to develop systems for detecting illicit financial flows (Target 16.4) and enhancing transparency in financial institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in computer science, mathematics, and statistics, directly supporting enabling technology (Target 17.8) and statistical capacity-building (Target 17.19) for SDG implementation.
